channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Police killings set record in 2024, but with smallest increase in years: Report
item.description: In 2024, police officers in the US killed 1,365 people, a slight increase from the 1,329 civilians killed in 2023, according to the Mapping Police Violence report.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Bloody crime scene photos play key role in trial of man accused in Palestinian American boy's death
item.description: JOLIET, Ill. (AP) — Photos of a child's bloody body, a black knife holder and other crime scene evidence took center stage Wednesday at the murder and hate crime trial for an Illinois landlord accused of fatally stabbing a 6-year-old Palestinian American boy in 2023.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Super Bowl sports reporter found dead in hotel had sedative in his system: authorities
item.description: The woman accused of taking the reporter's phone and bank cards has previously been accused of drugging men to steal from them, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ryan Reynolds' image takes hit amid Lively-Baldoni lawsuit; costars speak out
item.description: "Would I work with him again? No, I would not work with him again," said Reynolds' "Deadpool" co-star.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: ‘Concession Agreement’ with Venezuela will be terminated
item.description: In his post, Trump slammed the “Electoral conditions” within Venezuela and argued Maduro’s regime failed to meet the concessions given by Biden.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Immigration officials say everyone living in the US illegally must register: What to know
item.description: Federal immigration law has long required that people living illegally in the U.S. register with the government.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Judge weighs continued freedom for Adnan Syed in 'Serial' murder case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — More than a decade after Adnan Syed’s murder case attracted massive interest through the hit podcast “Serial,” a Baltimore judge is considering a request to reduce his sentence to time served, which would allow him to remain free indefinitely even though his conviction still stands.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mystery illnesses claim over 50 lives in Congo: What we know
item.description: Unidentified illnesses in northwestern Congo have killed more than 50 people over the past five weeks, nearly half of them within hours after they felt sick.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Judge criticizes Justice Department's broad reading of Trump's Capitol riot pardons
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday criticized the Justice Department's evolving position that a presidential pardon for a Kentucky man who stormed the Capitol also covers his conviction for illegally possessing guns at his home. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kroger giving out free boxes for Daylight Savings Time
item.description: FARMERSBURG, Ind. (WTWO/WAWV)— The time change is less than two weeks away and Kroger is hoping to make that change a little easier. Starting on March 4 at noon, Kroger will be giving away what they refer to as the Hour Back Box.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Boyll, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democratic governors pitch state jobs to federal workers cut by Trump
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — Some of America's governors — mostly Democrats — have a message for the wave of fired federal workers: We want you.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former NY detective moonlighted as Bonanno crime family soldier, helped fuel mini-mob war, feds allege
item.description: Hector Rosario, a former Nassau County detective, ‘sold himself’ to major crime clan Bonanno. He allegedly helped tip off members about multiple investigations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Christianity in US stops decline after years of losses: Survey
item.description: Although down substantially from 2007, 44 percent of Americans said they pray at least once a day, a figure that has remained consistent in the last four years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Musk dominates, disparages federal workers at first Trump Cabinet meeting
item.description: Elon Musk at the first meeting of President Trump’s Cabinet defended his email demanding all federal workers report their accomplishments to his office, calling it a “pulse check” and saying anyone with a heartbeat and neurons could complete it.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to rule for Ohio woman claiming job bias because she's straight
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court seemed likely Wednesday to side with an Ohio woman who claims she suffered sex discrimination from her employer because she is straight.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NFLPA report cards: Dolphins, Vikings finish at the top for second straight year
item.description: The Miami Dolphins are the top-ranked team, followed by the Minnesota Vikings, for the second consecutive season in the NFL Players Association report card.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Recent aviation disasters and close calls stoke fears about the safety of flying
item.description: All the recent aviation disasters and close calls have people worried about the safety of flying. The midair collision that killed 67 near Washington D.C. last month was the worst disaster.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump shares AI video envisioning Gaza development with gold statue of himself
item.description: The video also features children holding gold balloons also made to look like Trump and a renovated beachfront with luxury hotels and money flying in the air.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Officers cut open man's 'narco wig' at Colombian airport to reveal cocaine
item.description: He may have been using his head, but a 40-year-old passenger wasn't able to get past Colombian airport security with a cocaine-stuffed "narco wig," police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court to hear arguments on 'reverse discrimination' case
item.description: Marlean Ames filed suit against a state agency claiming she was not given a promotion because she is heterosexual.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, Fox 5

item.title: RFK Jr. targeting childhood psychiatric drugs; doctors push back
item.description: Childhood psychiatrists insist the drugs, for attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and depression, are nonaddictive and proven safe and say they are more concerned about young Americans unable to access psychiatric medications that could help.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KTLA 5

item.title: South Korea's Yoon defends his martial law decree as impeachment ruling nears
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In a final statement at his impeachment trial, South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol defended his martial law decree that plunged the country into chaos as a bid to inform the public of the danger of the opposition-controlled parliament as he vowed Tuesday to push for politi
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Millions of Social Security recipients are about to get back payments
item.description: More than 3.2 million Social Security recipients who received pensions from their time as teachers, firefighters, police officers and other public service jobs will soon see a boost in their benefits.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Missouri woman pleads guilty to federal charge in plot to sell Graceland
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — A Missouri woman pleaded guilty Tuesday to a federal charge accusing her of concocting a brazen plot to defraud Elvis Presley’s family by trying to auction off his Graceland mansion and property before a judge halted the mysterious foreclosure sale.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the Feb. 28 Economic Blackout
item.description: Customers across the United States are looking to send a message Friday to companies that have rolled back their DEI initiatives in the form of a 24-hour consumer boycott, also known as the “Feb. 28 Economic Blackout.
item.dc:creator: Daesha Gear, ABC 4

item.title: Michigan veterinarian faces theft charge after refusing to return homeless man's dog
item.description: GRAND RAPIDS, Mich. (AP) — A Michigan veterinarian who said she rescued a distressed dog that was tied to a truck is facing a theft charge after failing to return him to a homeless man.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A midair collision in Arizona prompts questions about air traffic control towers
item.description: Tens of thousands of planes take off, land and perform touch-and-goes at the Marana Regional Airport in southern Arizona every year. Without an air traffic control tower, it's a calculated dance that requires communication by pilots.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nearly 200K sign petition to revoke Musk's Canadian citizenship
item.description: The petition calls on Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to cancel Musk's citizenship and Canadian passport.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KRON4

item.title: Supreme Court orders new trial for Oklahoma death row inmate
item.description: The Supreme Court on Tuesday sided with an Oklahoma death row inmate who claimed alongside the state itself that his trial was unfair, ordering a new trial and staving off his execution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: What will win at a nailbiter Oscars? AP's film writers make their predictions
item.description: By the time the Oscars wind around after months of other award shows and constant handicapping, the prizes can feel almost predetermined. Not this year.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE and LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House Speaker Mike Johnson tries to push Trump's 'big' agenda forward, but GOP votes are in jeopardy
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson will try against the odds to muscle a Republican budget blueprint to passage this week, a step toward delivering President Donald Trump's “big, beautiful bill” with $4.5 trillion in tax breaks and $2 trillion in spending cuts over stiff opposition from Democrats — and even
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, KEVIN FREKING and MATT BROWN Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump backs Musk as he roils the federal workforce with demands and threats
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump backed Elon Musk's demand that federal employees explain their recent accomplishments by the end of Monday or risk getting fired, even as government agency officials were told that compliance with Musk's edict was voluntary.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4
